claim: Claude Code, Manus, and OpenClaw all share the same underlying architecture: model + runtime + harness; you can build open-source equivalents
actionable: Decompose agent systems into three layers (model, runtime, harness) to understand and replicate proprietary agent architectures.
source_title: Open Models, Open Runtime, Open Harness - Building your own AI agent with LangChain and Nvidia

claim: Nvidia's Nemotron 3 Super outperforms GPT-4o on both accuracy and speed; use open-source models in agents when you can host on your infrastructure
actionable: Consider open-source models like Nemotron for agent backends; they can match closed models and provide deployment flexibility.
source_title: Open Models, Open Runtime, Open Harness - Building your own AI agent with LangChain and Nvidia

claim: Nvidia OpenShell runtime enables policy-based security constraints on agent execution (e.g., block network access); layer permissions at runtime level
actionable: When building agents, use runtimes with explicit permission controls instead of relying solely on prompting for safety.
source_title: Open Models, Open Runtime, Open Harness - Building your own AI agent with LangChain and Nvidia

claim: Deep Agents harness auto-handles context engineering (summarization, window management) so you don't rebuild this for each project
actionable: Use LangChain Deep Agents or similar frameworks that abstract context management; don't build from scratch.
source_title: Open Models, Open Runtime, Open Harness - Building your own AI agent with LangChain and Nvidia
